reflection.theme: The Sunday You Get to Keep
reflection.quote: It is not enough to have a good mind; the main thing is to use it well.
reflection.reading:
Sundays used to be the worst day of the week. Not because anything happened on them, but because of what happened the night before — and the slow, sick math of trying to piece it back together while everyone else seemed to be brunching, hiking, calling their mom. Sunday was the day I lied to myself most. About how bad it had gotten. About whether I'd do it again next weekend. About what kind of person I was becoming.

Now Sunday is the day I trust most. It's the day that proves the week. I wake up clear, I know where my phone is, I know what I said and to whom. I can read for an hour without my brain skittering sideways. I can call someone back. I can go for a walk in the rain and actually be in the rain, not just enduring it on the way to feeling better. None of this is dramatic. That's the point.

Descartes was right that having a mind isn't the achievement — using it well is. Sobriety gave me my mind back, but the using-it-well part is daily work. It's the small choice to be here, in this Sunday, paying attention to what's actually in front of me instead of what I wish were different. The life is in the ordinary hours. Sundays especially.
reflection.meditation: Where can I be fully present today, in something small and ordinary?

affirmations: I am awake to my own life today, and that is not a small thing.
Today I choose presence over performance — I don't have to prove anything to be worthy of this day.
I am building a life I don't need to escape from, one ordinary Sunday at a time.

gratitude.prayer: I'm grateful for a Sunday that doesn't start with damage control. For coffee that tastes like coffee instead of penance. For the people who stayed in my life long enough to see me come back to it, and for the quiet kind of morning I used to think was reserved for other people. None of this was inevitable, and I don't forget that.
gratitude.closing: One more clear day. That's the whole gift.
